nodes.title: Validate the target before binding
nodes.why: `bind` only makes sense for callable values, so this gives a clear error early.
nodes.alternative: Skip the check and let `.apply` fail later.
nodes.tradeoff: More predictable error message with a tiny upfront check.

nodes.title: Choose this based on normal call vs new
nodes.why: When used with `new`, the fresh instance must be used as `this`, not the bound `thisArg`.
nodes.alternative: Always use `thisArg` no matter how the function is called.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more logic, but it matches real `bind` constructor behavior.

nodes.title: Preserve prototype chain for instanceof
nodes.why: This keeps instances from `new boundFn()` linked to the original function’s prototype.
nodes.tradeoff: Correct prototype behavior without aliasing the original prototype object.
